blocks.eyebrow: Why position needs its own mechanism
blocks.heading: Content similarity alone does not encode token order
paragraphs: Without positional information, self-attention compares token representations but does not by itself distinguish a permutation of those tokens. The original Transformer restores absolute index by adding a position vector. The relative-position line of work asks a different question: can the attention score see how far and in which direction two tokens are separated?

RoFormer treats that history as a design constraint rather than a list of baselines. Its Eq. 11 asks for query and key functions that receive absolute positions m and n while their inner product depends explicitly on m−n. Rotation is derived as an answer after this requirement is stated.

blocks.label: Paper's design question
blocks.question: Can absolute position m and n enter f_q and f_k while their inner product depends on the relative position m−n?
blocks.context: This requirement becomes Eq. 11. The paper also seeks sequence length flexibility, long-term decay, and compatibility with linear self-attention.

columns.label: Inputs to f_q and f_k
columns.question: What representations are constructed?
columns.answer: Functions receive a word embedding and its absolute position.

columns.label: Input to g
columns.question: What should the score retain?
columns.answer: The word embeddings and relative position m−n.

blocks.heading: Why this formulation matters
paragraphs: The paper does not begin by assuming that rotation is the answer. It first states a functional requirement for the query-key inner product, then derives a two-dimensional solution that can be generalized across the hidden dimension.

blocks.title: A paper-by-paper history of what position changes inside attention

stages.intent: Add sinusoidal absolute position before projection.
stages.change: Limitation carried forward: relative displacement is not explicit in the score.

stages.intent: Insert a learned relative-position representation into the compatibility term.
stages.change: Advance: displacement becomes explicit, but it is still added to a content key.

stages.intent: Decompose content–content, content–position, and global bias terms.
stages.change: Advance: relative interactions are explicit, but the score is still an additive decomposition.

stages.intent: Retain, simplify, or disentangle selected content and position interactions.
stages.change: RoFormer's shared diagnosis: each variant still edits terms in an additive decomposition.

stages.intent: Ask for absolute-position functions whose inner product depends on m−n.
stages.change: Design turn: state the relation before selecting the positional operator.

stages.intent: Pair dimensions and apply absolute rotations to query and key.
stages.change: Answer: two multiplicative absolute phases collapse to the relative phase R_{n−m}.

folded.reason: Proof and efficient-realization steps repeat the paired rotation; code mapping and appendix preserve them without thirteen near-duplicate clips.

blocks.title: Pause on the problem each predecessor solved
blocks.intro: The clip now holds each source long enough to read one equation and one limitation. The score remains the anchor; only the role of position changes.
blocks.observation: Sinusoidal encoding adds absolute position; Shaw adds a relative key; Transformer-XL decomposes score terms; later variants edit them; RoPE changes the operator.
blocks.consequence: Eq. 11 becomes the next scientific question: can absolute phases enter q and k while their dot product exposes m−n?

blocks.heading: Additive versus multiplicative
paragraphs: The paper describes prior position encodings as additive and presents RoPE as multiplicative: position acts through a transformation of query and key representations rather than only through a vector added to token content.

blocks.title: Further reading from the paper's context

items.title: Attention Is All You Need
items.relation: Sinusoidal absolute position encoding in the original Transformer.

items.title: Self-Attention with Relative Position Representations
items.relation: Relative position representations directly in self-attention.

items.title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context
items.relation: Relative positional attention for longer context recurrence.

items.title: DeBERTa: Decoding-enhanced BERT with Disentangled Attention
Rethinking Attention with Performers
items.relation: Linear self-attention context used in the paper's Performer experiment.

steps.label: Query input
steps.meaning: The query transformation receives embedding x_m and absolute position m.

steps.label: Key input
steps.meaning: The key transformation receives embedding x_n and absolute position n.

steps.label: Required relation
steps.meaning: Their inner product must expose relative position.

blocks.formula: (R_m W^q x_m)^T(R_n W^k x_n) = x_m^T W^{qT} R_{n−m} W^k x_n

steps.label: Query
steps.meaning: Equation 14 applies an absolute-position rotation to the query.

steps.label: Key
steps.meaning: The key receives the corresponding rotation for position n.

steps.label: Inner product
steps.meaning: Orthogonality produces the relative rotation in Eq. 16.

blocks.title: Why the score contains n−m
blocks.intro: The query and key still receive absolute-position rotations R_m and R_n. The relative term appears only when their inner product combines those rotations.
blocks.observation: Because each rotation is orthogonal, (R_m)^T R_n equals R_{n−m}.
blocks.consequence: The attention score depends on relative displacement even though q_m and k_n are constructed with their own absolute positions.

lines.maps_to: R_m q = q cos(mθ) + q_perp sin(mθ)
ordinary query-key dot product after rotation

blocks.heading: No dense rotary matrix is materialized
paragraphs: The negate-and-swap operation constructs a perpendicular companion for every adjacent coordinate pair. Elementwise cosine and sine then realize the same two-dimensional rotations in parallel.

blocks.question: Why does the paper claim long-term decay?
blocks.setting: Frequency-paired decomposition of the rotary attention score in Eqs. 35–37.
blocks.metric: A distance-dependent bound on the summed rotary contribution.
blocks.evidence: The derivation uses an Abel transformation and Figure 2 illustrates the reported decay. The animation is schematic, not digitized.
blocks.takeaway: The paper links increasing relative distance to reduced alignment across frequency-paired contributions.

blocks.title: From the frequency sum to a decay bound
blocks.intro: The formula stays visible while a point moves along a schematic distance-dependent bound.
blocks.observation: As |m−n| grows, the displayed bound decreases; the clip explicitly labels the curve as schematic.
blocks.consequence: This explains the direction of the paper's analysis without fabricating Figure 2 coordinates.

blocks.question: Is RoFormer uniformly better across GLUE?
blocks.setting: Six GLUE datasets under the paper's reported pre-training setup.
blocks.evidence: Higher on MRPC, STS-B, and QQP; lower on SST-2, QNLI, and MNLI in Table 2.
blocks.takeaway: The result is mixed rather than a universal improvement; signed deltas make that visible.

blocks.question: Does a longer input help on CAIL2019-SCM?
blocks.takeaway: The longer setting is higher, while the sequential training schedule prevents a pure causal attribution to length alone.

blocks.title: Mixed task deltas, then a longer-sequence comparison
blocks.intro: The clip first animates signed RoFormer−BERT deltas for GLUE, then changes the question and metric to CAIL2019-SCM test accuracy.
blocks.observation: GLUE contains positive and negative deltas. On CAIL2019-SCM, RoFormer-1024 is 1.50 points above RoFormer-512.
blocks.consequence: The page preserves both the mixed broad benchmark and the focused long-text result instead of selecting one flattering bar.

items.label: Language-model pre-training
items.finding: The paper reports that RoFormer converges faster than the BERT baseline in the shown pre-training experiment.

items.label: Performer with RoPE
items.finding: The paper reports rapid convergence and lower loss than Performer without RoPE in the shown experiment.

blocks.heading: Read each piece of evidence at its own level
paragraphs: Equation 16 establishes a relative-position identity. Table 1 provides one exact task comparison. Figure 3 supplies reported training trends. No single result substitutes for the others.

items.label: Exact algebra
items.detail: Equation 16 follows from the rotation construction and orthogonality.

items.label: Frequency schedule
items.detail: Long-term behavior depends on the selected frequencies and how the trained model uses them.

items.label: Optimization
items.detail: Convergence and downstream quality still depend on architecture, data, and training.

items.label: Extrapolation
items.detail: Relative position in the inner product does not by itself guarantee arbitrary-context generalization.

blocks.prompt: Does R_{n−m} mean q_m and k_n do not contain absolute position?
blocks.answer: No. q_m and k_n are individually rotated by m and n. Their inner product combines those rotations into the relative term.

appendix_entries.title: Source provenance
appendix_entries.body: Paper arXiv:2104.09864v5, PDF SHA-256 e9a481…af79. Official RoFormer repository revision dfc678ad…a9b4. Every new media file is hash checked.

appendix_entries.title: Related-work reading order
appendix_entries.body: Start with the Transformer's sinusoidal absolute encoding, then Shaw et al. and Transformer-XL for relative position, DeBERTa for disentangled attention, and Performer for the linear-attention setting used in the RoFormer experiments.

appendix_entries.title: From Eq. 11 to Eq. 16
appendix_entries.body: Equation 11 is the requirement. Equation 12 gives a two-dimensional complex-number solution; Eqs. 14-15 generalize it; Eq. 16 exposes relative position in the inner product.

appendix_entries.title: Formula, animation, and code map
appendix_entries.body: The new Manim source lives in scenes/explainer_pipeline_native.py. It joins Eq. 11, Eqs. 14-16, perpendicular-vector construction, cosine/sine application, and dot-product code without runtime code generation.

appendix_entries.title: Results reading guide
appendix_entries.body: The WMT chart uses exact Table 1 values and marks its truncated axis. Figure 3 statements are kept as the authors' reported trends rather than invented curve samples.

appendix_entries.title: Claim boundary
appendix_entries.body: The derivation establishes relative position inside the query-key score. It does not alone determine optimization quality, downstream performance, or arbitrary-context extrapolation.
